::: {.hero}

::: {.network-lines}
:::

::: {.dot .dot-1}
:::

::: {.dot .dot-2}
:::

::: {.dot .dot-3}
:::

::: {.dot .dot-4}
:::

::: {.dot .dot-5}
:::

::: {.dot .dot-6}
:::

::: {.page-container .hero-inner}

::: {.eyebrow}
SOCIAL EPIDEMIOLOGY · COMPLEXITY SCIENCE
:::

# How does health inequality emerge across a life?

I study how socioeconomic disadvantage shapes health across the life course, and why preventive healthcare systems often reach those who need them least.

::: {.button-row}
[My research](research.qmd){.button-primary}
[Publications](publications.qmd){.button-secondary}
:::

:::

:::

::: {.stats-band}

::: {.stat}
::: {.stat-number}
Life course
:::
::: {.stat-label}
inequalities
:::
:::

::: {.stat}
::: {.stat-number}
Health equity
:::
::: {.stat-label}
research
:::
:::

::: {.stat}
::: {.stat-number}
Preventive care
:::
::: {.stat-label}
systems
:::
:::

::: {.stat}
::: {.stat-number}
Complexity
:::
::: {.stat-label}
methods
:::
:::

:::

::: {.section}

::: {.page-container}

## Research strands

::: {.cards}

::: {.card .card-teal}

### Life-course inequalities

How socioeconomic disadvantage accumulates across lives and generations, shaping health and healthcare trajectories.

:::

::: {.card .card-rose}

### Preventive healthcare systems

How screening, monitoring, and routine care are organized — and why unequal uptake can emerge even in universal systems.

:::

::: {.card .card-gold}

### Complexity and health equity

Using complexity-informed approaches to study feedback, timing, adaptation, and unintended consequences in health systems.

:::

:::

:::

:::
